qa_pairs.question: What happens if a file is accidentally deleted from the `/nearline` directory on the Béluga cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: If you accidentally or deliberately delete a file from your `~/nearline` directory on the Béluga cluster, the tape copy of that file will be retained for up to 60 days.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user restore a deleted file from `/nearline` on the Béluga cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To restore a deleted file from `~/nearline` on the Béluga cluster, you need to contact technical support. You must provide them with the full path to the file(s) and specify the desired version by date, just as you would for restoring a backup.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to maintain a record of the directory structure for your `/nearline` space, and what command can help achieve this?
qa_pairs.answer: It is important to retain a copy of the complete directory structure of your `/nearline` space because you will need the full path for any file you wish to restore. You can create a record of all file locations by running the command `ls -R > ~/nearline_contents.txt` from your `~/nearline/PROJECT` directory.
